questions.q: In which year was the actor and filmmaker Kamal Haasan born?
questions.explanation: Kamal Haasan was born on 7 November 1954 in Paramakudi, Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: Kamal Haasan began his film career primarily as what kind of performer?
questions.explanation: Kamal Haasan started out as a child actor before becoming a leading man and filmmaker.

questions.q: In which 1987 Mani Ratnam film did Kamal Haasan play an underworld don in Bombay?
questions.explanation: Kamal Haasan starred as the don Velu Naicker in Mani Ratnam's acclaimed 1987 film Nayakan.

questions.q: In the 1996 film Indian, directed by S. Shankar, Kamal Haasan played a dual role of a father and son. What was the father character known as?
questions.explanation: Kamal Haasan played the freedom fighter Senapathy, popularly called Indian Thatha, alongside his son Chandru.

questions.q: In the 1996 comedy Avvai Shanmughi, Kamal Haasan disguised himself as which type of character?
questions.explanation: In Avvai Shanmughi, Kamal Haasan dressed as an elderly woman to stay close to his daughter.

questions.q: Across how many languages is Kamal Haasan especially noted for his acting work, including Tamil, Telugu, Hindi and others?
questions.explanation: Kamal Haasan has worked across Tamil, Telugu, Hindi, Malayalam, Kannada and other languages.

questions.q: Kamal Haasan is known not only as an actor but also prominently as which of the following?
questions.explanation: Kamal Haasan is celebrated as a versatile actor, screenwriter, producer and director.

questions.q: Kamal Haasan has won several of which major Indian government film honour during his career?
questions.explanation: Kamal Haasan has won multiple National Film Awards over his long career.

questions.q: In which Indian state is Kamal Haasan most strongly associated with the film industry where he became a star?
questions.explanation: Kamal Haasan rose to fame primarily in the Tamil film industry based in Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: Which political party did Kamal Haasan found in 2018, marking his entry into electoral politics?
questions.explanation: Kamal Haasan launched his political party Makkal Needhi Maiam in February 2018.

questions.q: Which 1992 film, directed by Bharathan and starring Kamal Haasan, was India's official entry to the Academy Awards and explored caste vendetta in a Tamil village?
questions.explanation: Thevar Magan (1992), written by and starring Kamal Haasan, was India's official Oscar entry that year.

questions.q: In the 1989 film Apoorva Sagodharargal, Kamal Haasan famously played a dwarf circus performer seeking revenge. What was that character's name?
questions.explanation: Kamal Haasan played the vertically challenged Appu in Apoorva Sagodharargal (1989).

questions.q: Which 1982 Tamil film directed by Balu Mahendra earned Kamal Haasan a National Film Award and depicted a man caring for a woman with childlike amnesia?
questions.explanation: Moondram Pirai (1982), remade in Hindi as Sadma, won Kamal Haasan a National Award for Best Actor.

questions.q: The Hindi film Sadma (1983), in which Kamal Haasan starred opposite Sridevi, was a remake of which of his own Tamil films?
questions.explanation: Sadma (1983) was the Hindi remake of Kamal Haasan's Tamil film Moondram Pirai.

questions.q: Which mentor and director gave Kamal Haasan many early opportunities and is considered his guru in Tamil cinema?
questions.explanation: K. Balachander mentored Kamal Haasan and directed several of his formative films.

questions.q: In the 1987 film Pushpaka Vimana (also known as Pushpak), what was notable about Kamal Haasan's performance?
options: It had no dialogue at all
questions.explanation: Pushpaka Vimana (1987) was a silent film with no spoken dialogue, starring Kamal Haasan.

questions.q: The 2000 film Hey Ram, written, produced and directed by Kamal Haasan, was set around which historical event?
options: The Partition of India and Gandhi's assassination
questions.explanation: Hey Ram (2000) was set against the backdrop of Partition and the assassination of Mahatma Gandhi.

questions.q: In the 2008 film Dasavathaaram, directed by K. S. Ravikumar, how many distinct roles did Kamal Haasan play?
questions.explanation: Kamal Haasan played ten different roles in Dasavathaaram (2008).

questions.q: Which K. Viswanath film, centred on classical dance, earned Kamal Haasan acclaim for his performance as a dancer?
questions.explanation: Sagara Sangamam (1983), directed by K. Viswanath, showcased Kamal Haasan as a gifted classical dancer.

questions.q: The 1986 Telugu film Swathi Muthyam, starring Kamal Haasan as an innocent, guileless man, was India's official entry to which awards?
questions.explanation: Swathi Muthyam (1986) was India's official entry for the Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film.

questions.q: Which Kamal Haasan espionage thriller, released in 2013 as a high-budget production he directed and starred in, sparked controversy before its release?
questions.explanation: Vishwaroopam (2013), directed by and starring Kamal Haasan, was a high-budget espionage thriller.

questions.q: In the 2022 blockbuster Vikram, directed by Lokesh Kanagaraj, Kamal Haasan played the title character. What was his character's name?
questions.explanation: Kamal Haasan played the lead agent Vikram in Lokesh Kanagaraj's 2022 action film of the same name.

questions.q: Which 1960 Tamil film, directed by A. Bhimsingh, marked Kamal Haasan's screen debut as a child actor?
questions.explanation: Kamal Haasan made his debut as a child actor in Kalathur Kannamma (1960).

questions.q: Which 1975 K. Balachander film, also marking the debut of Rajinikanth, gave Kamal Haasan a notable role as a young adult lead?
questions.explanation: Apoorva Raagangal (1975) featured Kamal Haasan in a key role and was also Rajinikanth's debut.

questions.q: In the 1981 film Raja Paarvai, which Kamal Haasan also produced, his lead character had what condition?
questions.explanation: In Raja Paarvai (1981), Kamal Haasan played a blind violinist, in a film he also produced.

questions.q: The 1996 film Indian, in which Kamal Haasan starred, featured music composed by which celebrated composer?
questions.explanation: A. R. Rahman composed the music for the 1996 film Indian directed by S. Shankar.

questions.q: Kamal Haasan's 1996 Tamil cross-dressing comedy Avvai Shanmughi was remade in Hindi the following year under which title, again starring him?
questions.explanation: Avvai Shanmughi (1996) was remade in Hindi as Chachi 420 (1997), both starring Kamal Haasan in drag.

questions.q: The civilian honour Padma Bhushan was conferred on Kamal Haasan by the Government of India in which year?
questions.explanation: Kamal Haasan received the Padma Bhushan in 2014, after the Padma Shri in 1990.

questions.q: In the 1995 thriller Kuruthipunal, a remake of the Hindi film Drohkaal, Kamal Haasan played which kind of character?
questions.explanation: In Kuruthipunal (1995), Kamal Haasan played a police officer who goes undercover to infiltrate a terrorist group.

questions.q: Kamal Haasan's 2003 film Anbe Sivam, which gained a strong reputation over time, paired him with which younger actor as a co-lead?
questions.explanation: Anbe Sivam (2003) co-starred Kamal Haasan with R. Madhavan and was directed by Sundar C.
